title: Constructing /k/ substitution intervention priority
content: For persistent substitution on target sounds, stabilize the sound in initial position first, then expand to medial or phrase-level carryover after accuracy improves.

title: Final consonant omission support
content: When final-position omissions are frequent, emphasize auditory contrast, slower segmented production, and explicit retention of the final target sound before increasing speech rate.

title: Minimal pairs for substitution contrast
content: Use minimal-pair contrast when the child confuses a target sound with an already established sound. Keep the pair set small and emphasize meaning differences.

title: Multisyllabic word retention shaping
content: If target sounds collapse in multisyllabic words, split the word into syllable chunks first, then rebuild the full word while preserving the target sound in each attempt.

title: Home practice routine for target sounds
content: Use short, high-frequency practice in games or daily routines, reinforce accurate productions immediately, and avoid repeated high-pressure correction.
